stages.name: Core Verbs & Pronouns
stages.goal: Produce basic 'Subject + verb + object' sentences using core action verbs and pronouns
stages.grammar_focus: Vietnamese sentence order is Subject + Verb + Object, same as English. Negation: add 'không' before the verb.

stages.name: Numbers & Time
stages.goal: Produce sentences with numbers, days of the week, months, and time expressions
stages.grammar_focus: Time expressions typically come at the start or end of a sentence. 'Vào' means 'on' for days/months. Time: [number] + giờ.

stages.name: Question Words
stages.goal: Form correct questions using Vietnamese question words with proper placement
stages.grammar_focus: Question word placement rules: When (khi nào) — start or end. Why (tại sao) — start. Who (ai) — end if verb present, before noun if no verb. What (gì) — end. How (thế nào) — end. Where (ở đâu) — end. Yes/no questions: Bạn có [verb] không?

stages.name: Tense Markers
stages.goal: Produce past, present-continuous, and future sentences using đã / đang / sẽ
stages.grammar_focus: Vietnamese tense markers come BEFORE the main verb. Đã = past (already done). Đang = present continuous (currently happening). Sẽ = future (will happen). 'Rồi' at the end strengthens past completion: Tôi đã ăn rồi.

stages.name: Connectors
stages.goal: Produce compound and causal sentences using conjunctions and connective words
stages.grammar_focus: Conjunctions mostly follow English word order. 'Nếu' (if) begins a clause. 'Nên' means both 'so' (consequence) and 'should'. 'Sau đó' is always between two complete clauses.

stages.name: Descriptive Vocabulary
stages.goal: Produce descriptive sentences about colors, weather, seasons, and emotions
stages.grammar_focus: Adjectives in Vietnamese follow the noun they modify: 'hoa đỏ' (red flower), not 'đỏ hoa'. Weather: 'Trời + [adjective]' or 'Có + [noun]'. Emotions: Subject + [emotion word], no 'to be' needed.

stages.name: Free Composition
stages.goal: Produce complex multi-clause sentences and short paragraphs drawing on all previous vocabulary
stages.grammar_focus: Review of all previous grammar. Focus on combining tense markers + connectors + descriptive vocab in one sentence. Practice maintaining correct word order across clause boundaries.

stages.name: Places & Daily Life
stages.goal: Describe where you live, how far places are, and talk about everyday situations like visiting and birthdays
stages.grammar_focus: Location: 'Ở' before place means 'at/in'. Distance: A + cách + B + [number + unit] (e.g. Nhà tôi cách đây 5km). 'Gần' (near) and 'Đó' (there) follow the noun or stand alone. 'Về thăm' is a fixed phrase meaning to return for a visit.

stages.name: Family & Relationships
stages.goal: Name family members and close relationships, and use kin terms as pronouns in conversation
stages.grammar_focus: Vietnamese kin terms double as pronouns: speak to parents as 'Con' (I) + 'Ba/Mẹ' (you). Possessives: [kin term] + 'của' + [pronoun], often shortened: 'mẹ tôi' (my mother). Extended family uses paternal/maternal suffixes: nội = dad's side, ngoại = mom's side.
